Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for wire fraud correspondence using search_emails (query: "wire fraud" or "Pham" or "STOP" or "breach review" or similar). The agent discovers: (a) Sofia's "STOP - DO NOT WIRE" email sent to the borrower CC'd to barbara.hensley@tryontitle.com, (b) Grace Yamamoto's email to Denise Holloway with subject "Need breach review on Pham wire fraud file" stating Sofia was the last legitimate Keystone sender before the spoofed message and requesting assessment of vendor impersonation vs mailbox compromise, and (c) Denise Holloway's response email with subject "Pham file: breach assessment started" confirming compliance review is underway and both possibilities (vendor spoofing only vs Sofia's mailbox/session compromise) are being treated as open. The wire fraud file is LN-2026-00605 with closing at Tryon Title.
OE 2: Search emails for the missed insurance binder condition and realtor escalation using search_emails (query: "Allison Mercer" or "insurance binder" or "PTC conditions" or "closing failure" or similar). The agent discovers: (a) the lender condition email from conditions@homesteadwholesale.com to Sofia requesting an updated HOI binder naming Homestead Wholesale Lending as mortgagee by 3/17 for LN-2026-00616, (b) Allison Mercer's email (allison.mercer@mercerrealtygroup.com) with subject "Destiny Pham closing failure" threatening counsel and broker escalation, and (c) Grace Yamamoto's reply to Allison confirming she is taking over escalation and working with the lender on reinstatement/lock recovery.
OE 3: Search emails for the deposit documentation issue and the redisclosure/term change using search_emails (query: "$14K" or "deposit" or "Facebook Marketplace" or "redisclosure" or "30-year to 15-year" or "LN-2026-00613" or similar). The agent discovers: (a) emails about the $14K cash deposit condition on LN-2026-00610 where the borrower's LOX (letter of explanation) was deemed weak, and (b) Denise Holloway's email to Sofia with subject "LN-2026-00613 redisclosure timing" CC'd to nadia.osman@keystonemortgage.com confirming that the loan term change from 30yr to 15yr requires corrected disclosures and a new 3-business-day waiting period, with instructions to keep the file on hold.
OE 4: Search Slack messages for wire fraud and closing incidents using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "wire" or "LN-2026-00605" or "spoofed" or similar). The agent discovers messages in #closings (C003) including: (a) Sofia flagging the fraudulent wire instructions, "Real Barbara at Tryon just confirmed they did NOT send any updated wiring," (b) Grace activating incident mode, "Pulling this into incident mode. For LN-2026-00605: Sofia was last legit Keystone sender before the spoofed wire email hit," and (c) Sofia confirming she sent the STOP email but the borrower says the wire already went out.
OE 5: Search Slack messages for the missed condition and pipeline review using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "LN-2026-00616" or "suspended" or "pipeline review" or similar). The agent discovers messages in #loan-processing (C002) including: (a) Sofia admitting "Just opened LN-2026-00616 and realized the 3/17 cond never went back, file is suspended, lock dies 3/23, and Destiny's closing already passed. I missed this," (b) Camille Foster flagging the lock expiration, "LN-2026-00616 just flipped suspended on my lock list. Lock shows 3/23 exp and this won't clear before then," (c) Carlos Rivera reporting Allison's escalation, (d) Grace directing Elena to take over the file and scheduling Sofia's pipeline review for the next morning.
OE 6: Search Slack messages for the deposit documentation discussion using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "LN-2026-00610" or "$14k" or "Facebook Marketplace" or similar). The agent discovers messages in #loan-processing (C002) including: (a) Sofia asking Elena "@Elena quick gut check on LN-2026-00610 -- borrower says the $14k cash dep was from selling furniture on Facebook Marketplace but she has no receipts. Have you seen UW take screenshots/chat history + LOX on something like that?", (b) Elena Marchetti's reply: "Need more than just the letter. Best shot is FB Marketplace listing screenshots, Messenger convos, anything showing item/prices, maybe pics of the furniture and a written sale breakdown. Cash is the hard part though," and (c) Camille flagging that LN-2026-00610 has a 3/23 lock expiration and is still sitting in conditional approval.
OE 7: Search Slack messages for the 30yr-to-15yr term switch and redisclosure issue using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "LN-2026-00613" or "30yr" or "15yr" or "redisclosure" or similar). The agent discovers messages in #loan-processing (C002) including: (a) Sofia discovering the disclosure gap, "Doing closing prep on LN-2026-00613 and the CD APR is lining up to the 15yr terms, but the LE in file is still the old 30yr one," (b) Denise Holloway confirming, "if CD APR on LN-2026-00613 is outside tolerance vs the last valid LE, we have to reset. Since no revised LE went out after the 15yr switch, assume new 3 biz day wait once corrected disclosure is issued," (c) Carlos sending the borrower a delay notification, and (d) Camille flagging the lock, "Pham lock on LN-2026-00613 still shows 3/23 exp and now closing is landing after the reset window. Looks like we need extension pricing ASAP."
OE 8: Search the Mortgage LOS for the affected loans using mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "Pham" or "Destiny" or similar) and/or mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00605", "LN-2026-00610", "LN-2026-00613", "LN-2026-00616") to retrieve loan details including internal loan IDs needed for activity notes. The agent discovers: (a) LN-2026-00605 (id: los_loan_e0934521fda4), status "clear_to_close", loan amount $268,000, closing date 2026-04-11, (b) LN-2026-00610 (id: los_loan_3662f4616df5), status "processing", loan amount $305,000, (c) LN-2026-00613 (id: los_loan_4af68a782108), status "conditional_approval", loan amount $433,000, and (d) LN-2026-00616 (id: los_loan_66db84a42390), status "processing", loan amount $290,000.
OE 9: Search CRM engagements for notes related to the deposit condition and term switch using crm_list_engagements or crm_search_deals (or similar). The agent discovers CRM engagement notes including: (a) "UW added large deposit cond. $14k cash dep needs LOX and any support" tied to LN-2026-00610, (b) "Nadia called Carlos to confirm she updated Destiny Pham to 15yr fixed in the LOS", confirming the term change was entered but no revised LE was sent, and (c) Camille's call note about the lock expiration and extension pricing on LN-2026-00613.
OE 10: Look up Carlos Rivera's contact information using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Carlos Rivera" or "Carlos" or similar) to retrieve his email address carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com for the email write action.
OE 11: Add a status note to the wire fraud loan LN-2026-00605 using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_e0934521fda4", action: "note" or similar, detail: summary of wire fraud status, breach review underway by Denise, borrower wired funds to fraudulent account, Sofia's mailbox under assessment, closing on hold pending incident resolution).
OE 12: Add a status note to the missed condition loan LN-2026-00616 using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_66db84a42390", action: "note" or similar, detail: summary of missed condition status, HOI binder condition missed 3/17 deadline, file suspended, lock expired 3/23, closing missed, realtor Allison Mercer threatening counsel, Grace took over escalation, file reassigned to Elena for lender cleanup).
OE 13: Add a status note to the deposit condition loan LN-2026-00610 using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_3662f4616df5", action: "note" or similar, detail: summary of deposit documentation status, $14K cash deposit flagged by UW, borrower claims Facebook Marketplace furniture sale with no receipts, Elena advised screenshots/Messenger convos/sale breakdown as best documentation approach, lock expiring 3/23, Carlos handling borrower communication).
OE 14: Add a status note to the redisclosure loan LN-2026-00613 using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_4af68a782108", action: "note" or similar, detail: summary of redisclosure status, borrower switched 30yr to 15yr, no revised LE issued after term change, CD APR outside tolerance vs old LE, Denise confirmed need for corrected disclosures and new 3-business-day wait, Nadia copied for disclosure rework, lock shows 3/23 expiration and may need extension pricing).
OE 15: Send an email to Carlos Rivera about the deposit documentation situation using send_email (sender: "sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com", recipients: ["carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com"], subject: related to deposit documentation or LN-2026-00610 status or similar, content: update on where things stand with the $14K deposit, Elena's guidance that screenshots, Messenger convos, and a written sale breakdown are the best path forward, the lock clock is running with 3/23 expiration, and asking Carlos to push the borrower for the backup documentation before the lock dies).
OE 16: Post a summary message in #loan-processing using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002", payload: brief update covering the status of all four problem files, wire fraud file on hold pending breach review, missed condition file reassigned to Elena for lender cleanup, deposit documentation file awaiting borrower backup per Elena's guidance with lock at risk, and redisclosure file on hold for corrected LE with lock extension needed).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for wire fraud correspondence using search_emails (query: "wire fraud" or "Pham" or "STOP" or "breach review" or similar). The agent discovers: (a) Sofia's "STOP - DO NOT WIRE" email sent to the borrower CC'd to barbara.hensley@tryontitle.com, (b) Grace Yamamoto's email to Denise Holloway with subject "Need breach review on Pham wire fraud file" stating Sofia was the last legitimate Keystone sender before the spoofed message and requesting assessment of vendor impersonation vs mailbox compromise, and (c) Denise Holloway's response email with subject "Pham file: breach assessment started" confirming compliance review is underway and both possibilities (vendor spoofing only vs Sofia's mailbox/session compromise) are being treated as open. The wire fraud file is LN-2026-00605 with closing at Tryon Title.
OE 2: Search emails for the missed insurance binder condition and realtor escalation using search_emails (query: "Allison Mercer" or "insurance binder" or "PTC conditions" or "closing failure" or similar). The agent discovers: (a) the lender condition email from conditions@homesteadwholesale.com to Sofia requesting an updated HOI binder naming Homestead Wholesale Lending as mortgagee by 3/17 for LN-2026-00616, (b) Allison Mercer's email (allison.mercer@mercerrealtygroup.com) with subject "Destiny Pham closing failure" threatening counsel and broker escalation, and (c) Grace Yamamoto's reply to Allison confirming she is taking over escalation and working with the lender on reinstatement/lock recovery.
OE 3: Search emails for the deposit documentation issue and the redisclosure/term change using search_emails (query: "$14K" or "deposit" or "Facebook Marketplace" or "redisclosure" or "30-year to 15-year" or "LN-2026-00613" or similar). The agent discovers: (a) emails about the $14K cash deposit condition on LN-2026-00610 where the borrower's LOX (letter of explanation) was deemed weak, and (b) Denise Holloway's email to Sofia with subject "LN-2026-00613 redisclosure timing" CC'd to nadia.osman@keystonemortgage.com confirming that the loan term change from 30yr to 15yr requires corrected disclosures and a new 3-business-day waiting period, with instructions to keep the file on hold.
OE 4: Search Slack messages for wire fraud and closing incidents using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "wire" or "LN-2026-00605" or "spoofed" or similar). The agent discovers messages in #closings (C003) including: (a) Sofia flagging the fraudulent wire instructions, "Real Barbara at Tryon just confirmed they did NOT send any updated wiring," (b) Grace activating incident mode, "Pulling this into incident mode. For LN-2026-00605: Sofia was last legit Keystone sender before the spoofed wire email hit," and (c) Sofia confirming she sent the STOP email but the borrower says the wire already went out.
OE 5: Search Slack messages for the missed condition and pipeline review using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "LN-2026-00616" or "suspended" or "pipeline review" or similar). I missed this," (b) Camille Foster flagging the lock expiration, "LN-2026-00616 just flipped suspended on my lock list. Lock shows 3/23 exp and this won't clear before then," (c) Carlos Rivera reporting Allison's escalation, (d) Grace directing Elena to take over the file and scheduling Sofia's pipeline review for the next morning.
OE 6: Search Slack messages for the deposit documentation discussion using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "LN-2026-00610" or "$14k" or "Facebook Marketplace" or similar). The agent discovers messages in #loan-processing (C002) including: (a) Sofia asking Elena "@Elena quick gut check on LN-2026-00610 -- borrower says the $14k cash dep was from selling furniture on Facebook Marketplace but she has no receipts. Have you seen UW take screenshots/chat history + LOX on something like that?", (b) Elena Marchetti's reply: "Need more than just the letter. OE 7: Search Slack messages for the 30yr-to-15yr term switch and redisclosure issue using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "LN-2026-00613" or "30yr" or "15yr" or "redisclosure" or similar). Looks like we need extension pricing ASAP."
OE 8: Search the Mortgage LOS for the affected loans using mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "Pham" or "Destiny" or similar) and/or mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00605", "LN-2026-00610", "LN-2026-00613", "LN-2026-00616") to retrieve loan details including internal loan IDs needed for activity notes. OE 14: Add a status note to the redisclosure loan LN-2026-00613 using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_4af68a782108", action: "note" or similar, detail: summary of redisclosure status, borrower switched 30yr to 15yr, no revised LE issued after term change, CD APR outside tolerance vs old LE, Denise confirmed need for corrected disclosures and new 3-business-day wait, Nadia copied for disclosure rework, lock shows 3/23 expiration and may need extension pricing).
OE 15: Send an email to Carlos Rivera about the deposit documentation situation using send_email (sender: "sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com", recipients: ["carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com"], subject: related to deposit documentation or LN-2026-00610 status or similar, content: update on where things stand with the $14K deposit, Elena's guidance that screenshots, Messenger convos, and a written sale breakdown are the best path forward, the lock clock is running with 3/23 expiration, and asking Carlos to push the borrower for the backup documentation before the lock dies).
OE 16: Post a summary message in #loan-processing using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002", payload: brief update covering the status of all four problem files, wire fraud file on hold pending breach review, missed condition file reassigned to Elena for lender cleanup, deposit documentation file awaiting borrower backup per Elena's guidance with lock at risk, and redisclosure file on hold for corrected LE with lock extension needed).

Rubric Results

1 A loan activity note was added (via mortgage_los_add_activity) to the loan with loan number LN-2026-00605.
outcome
